title: triticum aestivum f. albidum
intro: Triticum aestivum f. albidum, also known as white wheat, is a variant of the common wheat plant (Triticum aestivum) that is characterized by its pale or white coloration. The medicinal properties of white wheat include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of consuming white wheat include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve gut health, and provide essential nutrients such as fiber and iron. White wheat can be prepared as an herbal tea or added to food as a nutritious and flavorful ingredient, with some herbal preparations also involving the use of its flour as a natural thickening agent.
